title: hieracium alatum subsp. cuneatum
intro: Hieracium alatum subsp. cuneatum, also known as winged hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its distinctive wing-like leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium alatum subsp. cuneatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential skin irritation and allergic reactions.
